description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was stopped for a red traffic light on northbound Howard Street while preparing to turn left onto 
westbound 12th Street in San Francisco. After the light turned green the Zoox vehicle began to move forward and then slowed gradually to 
yield to a pedestrian with right of way in the crosswalk on 12th Street. Just as the Zoox vehicle slowed to almost a stop (0.2 mph), a 
skateboarder quickly approached the rear of the Zoox vehicle extending their left arm forward and making contact with the right rear corner 
of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy and brought the vehicle to a stop. The skateboarder initially 
told the Zoox vehicle operator they were not injured but later mentioned they “smacked” their hand and it was “bruised.” When asked, 
however, they declined medical attention. The Zoox vehicle did not sustain damage. The police were not called. The Zoox vehicle camera 
footage shows that the skateboarder was looking at  their cell phone and did not appear to have been looking ahead at the roadway before the
 time of contact.
Print
Clear Form
